topic: Sharp non-equivalence theorem for local-projection (LP) versus structural-vector-autoregression (SVAR) identification of structural impulse responses under non-Gaussian innovations and partial sign restrictions.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the sharp algebraic boundary under which LP-IRFs and cumulant-identified SVAR-IRFs remain the same population functional when shocks are non-Gaussian and only partial sign/zero restrictions are imposed.

source_refs.claim: LPs and VARs estimate the same population impulse responses under unrestricted lag structures and the same identifying restrictions, including sign restrictions.
source_refs.author: Plagborg-Moller and Wolf

source_refs.claim: SVAR non-identifiability disappears if structural shocks are independent and at most one is Gaussian, so ICA can recover structural shocks without conventional restrictions.
source_refs.author: Gourieroux, Monfort, and Renne

source_refs.claim: The Gaussian case is exceptional; independent non-Gaussian SVAR errors identify essentially unique impulse responses without additional restrictions.
source_refs.author: Lanne, Meitz, and Saikkonen

source_refs.excerpt: the diagnostic margin \\(\\chi_w(P)\\) follows from fourth-cumulant geometry and partial sign restrictions
prove Conjecture 1 as a primitive open semi-algebraic strict-gap theorem

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published LP-VAR equivalence is stated for common structural restrictions on the IRF estimand, while ICA/non-Gaussian SVAR identification selects an impact rotation through higher cumulants of reduced-form innovations. The parent proposal collapsed because it assumed a positive quantitative margin by `A-quant-margin`; reviewers required that margin to be derived from fourth-cumulant geometry and partial sign restrictions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem giving explicit rank/transversality conditions on `(Gamma_h, Sigma_u, K_u, A, Z, w)` under which the LP maximizer is separated from the cumulant-diagonalizing algebraic locus, plus the matching condition that recovers equality.

open_problems.open_problem: Find the exact cumulant-matching restriction that restores LP/SVAR equivalence at the Gaussian boundary and gives sharp zero-gap cases for bounded but nonzero fourth-cumulant gaps.

source_refs.claim: Standard non-Gaussian SVAR inference has incorrect coverage when deviations from Gaussianity are small; locally robust methods are needed near local-to-Gaussian densities.
source_refs.author: Hoesch, Lee, and Mesters

source_refs.claim: Higher-moment SVAR identification imposes strong shock-process assumptions, should be tested, and weak identification is a priority for applied users.
source_refs.author: Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, and Qian

source_refs.excerpt: whether the reflected component of O(2) is identified with the same target-column angle set

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing work treats the Gaussian boundary as weak identification for non-Gaussian SVARs, not as an equality frontier between LP-identified and cumulant-identified structural IRF functionals. The CausalSmith two-shock witness also left reflected `O(2)` components and boundary cases unresolved.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A closed-form two-shock formula for `h_LP-h_SVAR` over both rotation and reflection components, with sharp zero-gap cases for Gaussian, equal-kurtosis, pinned-sign, flat-report, and cumulant-matching boundaries.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize when LP and SVAR identified sets coincide, strictly include one another, or only intersect after partial sign/zero restrictions are combined with bounded higher-cumulant inequalities.

source_refs.claim: SVARs with sign and zero restrictions require careful posterior algorithms; penalty-function approaches can add restrictions beyond the stated identification scheme.
source_refs.author: Arias, Rubio-Ramirez, and Waggoner

source_refs.claim: Adding independence to sign restrictions can reject many rotations, but confidence sets may be non-convex and require large samples or strong non-normality to narrow much.
source_refs.author: Drautzburg and Wright

source_refs.claim: Higher-order moment inequality restrictions combined with sign restrictions can considerably narrow SVAR identified sets relative to minimal sign restrictions.
source_refs.author: Andrade, Ferroni, and Melosi
source_refs.venue: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston Working Paper

source_refs.excerpt: Define c_w(P) as a canonical extremal value

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published set-identification literature works mainly in SVAR rotation space and does not compare the LP-implied set with the SVAR-implied set after cumulant inequalities are imposed. The failed upgrade similarly asserted a mechanism diagnostic but did not give a canonical finite optimization formula for the comparison set.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A pair of semi-algebraic formulas for `S_LP(h)` and `S_SVAR(h)` as images of orthogonal rotations satisfying sign/zero and cumulant inequalities, with necessary-and-sufficient conditions for equality, strict inclusion, disjointness, and non-convexity.

open_problems.open_problem: State and prove a non-tautological iff frontier for strict LP/SVAR non-equivalence using setwise zero-channel conditions rather than infimum diagnostics alone.

source_refs.excerpt: The zero-channel conclusion is setwise, not inferred from the infimum diagnostics alone
Rewrite Conjecture 2 so the iff uses the setwise zero-channel quantities directly

open_problems.why_unsolved: The reviewer found the sign channel partly automatic under the current normalization and the Plagborg-Moller-Wolf reduction not literal unless the LP side receives the same cumulant restriction. The missing obstacle is a setwise contact condition that avoids proving strictness from an infimum diagnostic by definition.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An iff theorem: `Delta_w^H(P)>0` iff every exposed LP maximizer fails the relevant common-impact or cumulant-contact condition, with a corrected common-restriction LP set recovering PMW at zero gap.

open_problems.open_problem: Turn the shock-masquerading angle into an operational threshold theorem that excludes support ties between SVAR and non-SVAR LP maximizers.

source_refs.excerpt: non-Gaussian SVAR/sign identify distinct structural impulse-response functionals exactly when
Redefine the exposed masquerading set as M_w^+(P)

open_problems.why_unsolved: The rejected angle falsely equated existence of a non-SVAR LP maximizer with a positive support gap; the reviewer noted that an SVAR maximizer can tie at the same support value. The unresolved piece is a threshold or exposed-contact condition that rules out ties.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A threshold theorem based on `M_w^+(P)={Q in argmax_LP F_w: Q notin Q_SVAR, g_w(Q;P)>0}` plus a finite polynomial program for `tau_w^*(P)` and a no-tie contact-locus boundary.

open_problems.open_problem: Extend LP lag-misspecification robustness results to structural identification rules that depend on estimated higher cumulants of residuals rather than only second-order projection objects.

source_refs.claim: LP confidence intervals retain coverage under local VAR misspecification, while short-to-moderate lag VAR confidence intervals can severely undercover.
source_refs.author: Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf

source_refs.claim: LP and VAR estimators share the same estimand, but VARs should use long lag lengths to ensure equivalence with LP when the goal is robust uncertainty assessment.
source_refs.author: Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf

source_refs.claim: Local statistical identification of SVARs can be obtained through third and fourth unconditional moments, with rank tests for skewness and kurtosis conditions.
source_refs.author: Guay

open_problems.why_unsolved: Misspecified-lag LP theory controls IRF inference for projection coefficients, whereas higher-cumulant identification uses residual transformations whose probability limits under lag truncation can differ from the structural innovation cumulants. The literature has not supplied the bias map from truncated projections to third- or fourth-cumulant identifying tensors.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sufficient-condition theorem or impossibility result for consistency of cumulant tensors estimated from LP residuals under local lag misspecification, with an explicit fourth-cumulant bias term.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop weak-identification robust inference for the difference between LP-identified and non-Gaussian SVAR-identified IRFs under local-to-Gaussian shocks.

source_refs.claim: Locally robust semiparametric inference exploits non-Gaussianity when present but maintains correct size and coverage under local-to-Gaussian densities.
source_refs.author: Hoesch, Lee, and Mesters

source_refs.claim: Weak non-Gaussianity causes size distortions in simultaneous-equation inference; locally robust semiparametric methods improve coverage.
source_refs.author: Lee and Mesters

source_refs.claim: Finite-sample inference from higher moments is demanding, and weak identification should be a priority.
source_refs.author: Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, and Qian

open_problems.why_unsolved: Robust non-Gaussian SVAR inference exists for SVAR structural functions, but not for the null that LP and SVAR structural IRFs are the same functional when the cumulants doing identification are weak or local to zero.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A weak-identification robust confidence set for `Delta_h = IRF_LP,h - IRF_SVAR,h`, valid uniformly over Gaussian, local-to-Gaussian, and strongly non-Gaussian shock distributions.

literature_map:
Jorda (2005, AER, jorda2005lp) introduced LPs as direct IRF regressions, emphasizing misspecification robustness relative to iterated VAR IRFs.
Plagborg-Moller and Wolf (2021, Econometrica, plagborgmoller_wolf2021_lpvar) prove LPs and VARs have the same population IRF estimand under unrestricted lag structures and common identifying restrictions, including sign restrictions.
Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf (2024/2026, NBER/Econometrica revision, mopqw2024_double_robustness) shift the LP-VAR comparison to misspecified finite-lag VARs: LP CIs retain coverage under local misspecification, while short-lag VAR CIs can undercover.
Montiel Olea et al. (2025, NBER Macro Annual, mopqw2025_primer) summarize the emerging consensus: same estimand asymptotically, but a bias-variance and lag-length robustness tradeoff in samples.
Gourieroux, Monfort, and Renne (2017, Journal of Econometrics, gmr2017_ica_svar) and Lanne, Meitz, and Saikkonen (2017, Journal of Econometrics, lms2017_nongaussian_svar) are anchor papers for non-Gaussian SVAR identification via independent components.
Lanne and Luoto (2021, JBES, lanne_luoto2021_gmm) relax full independence toward orthogonality plus co-kurtosis restrictions and identify shocks locally by GMM moments.
Guay (2021, Journal of Econometrics, guay2021_higher_moments) gives local statistical identification conditions using third and fourth unconditional moments, with partial identification of parameter subsets when the whole system is not identified.
Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, and Qian (2022, AEA P&P, mopq2022_higher_moments) warn that higher-moment SVAR identification imposes strong shock-process assumptions, should be tested, and is vulnerable to weak identification in finite samples.
Drautzburg and Wright (2023, Journal of Econometrics, drautzburg_wright2023_independence) use independence to refine sign-restricted identified sets without assuming higher moments point-identify the model; sets may become non-convex.
Hoesch, Lee, and Mesters (2024, Quantitative Economics, hoesch_lee_mesters2024_lr_svar) develop weak-non-Gaussianity robust inference for non-Gaussian SVARs, showing standard methods fail near Gaussianity.
Andrade, Ferroni, and Melosi (2025, FRB Boston WP, andrade_ferroni_melosi2025_hom_ineq) combine higher-moment inequality restrictions with sign restrictions and show large identified-set narrowing, but not a LP-SVAR equivalence boundary.
Arias, Rubio-Ramirez, and Waggoner (2018, Econometrica, arias_rr_waggoner2018_signzero) provide Bayesian inference algorithms for SVARs under sign and zero restrictions and show penalty-function approaches can add unintended restrictions.
Baumeister and Hamilton (2015, Econometrica, baumeister_hamilton2015_sign_priors) show sign-restricted SVARs can remain underidentified asymptotically, so prior mass inside the identified set drives conclusions.
The unresolved tension is that LP-VAR equivalence is framed around common IRF estimands and restrictions, while non-Gaussian SVAR work identifies rotations through distributional cumulants of shocks; the literature has not pinned down when those cumulant-based structural functionals are also LP-identifiable.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined parent bank README at CausalSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/downgraded/eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_v1/README.md; accepted parent directory was absent, downgraded parent carried the direct signal.
Mined parent proposal eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_v1_proposal.tex; yielded primitive cumulant-gap, two-shock witness, and scope-flag open problems.
Mined parent Stage 0 derivation reviews stage_0.5_to_0_attempt{1,2}.json; yielded the main collapse reason: strict gap assumed via A-quant-margin.
Mined rejected parent angles angle0 and angle1; yielded horizonwise/common-shock and orthogonal-block common-completion issues.
Mined upgrade bank README at CausalSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/failed/eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade/README.md; yielded mechanism-upgrade survival plus soundness blockers.
Mined upgrade proposal eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade_proposal.tex; yielded three-channel factorization and iff-frontier open problems.
Mined upgrade reviews angle3_v1.json and angle4_v3.json; yielded masquerading-threshold and canonical-diagnostic repair paths.
Active research/eid_lp_svar_nonequiv contained only upgrade2 state/pipeline files, no proposal text to mine.
